title: A resolution honoring Mississippi's Gestational Age Act.
plain_summary: This resolution honors Mississippi State Representative Becky Currie for creating a law that banned most abortions after 15 weeks of pregnancy. Her law led to a big Supreme Court case called Dobbs versus Jackson Women's Health Organization in 2022. The Supreme Court's decision overturned two older court cases called Roe versus Wade and Planned Parenthood versus Casey. The Senate wants to thank Currie and honor other states that have made similar laws to protect unborn babies.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary. (text: CR S302-303)

sections.title: Resolution Honoring Mississippi's Gestational Age Act
sections.text: In January 2018, Mississippi State Representative Becky Currie from Brookhaven, Mississippi introduced a law called the Gestational Age Act. This law was designed to protect unborn babies by preventing elective abortions after fifteen weeks of pregnancy. In March 2018, the Mississippi State Legislature passed this law, Governor Phil Bryant approved it, and it became official state law. In May 2021, the United States Supreme Court agreed to review a court case called Dobbs versus Jackson Women's Health. This case challenged whether the Gestational Age Act was allowed under the Constitution. The main question was whether all laws that ban elective abortions before a baby can survive outside the womb are against the Constitution. On June 24, 2022, the United States Supreme Court made a historic decision in the Dobbs case. The Court ruled that the Constitution does not give people a right to abortion. The Court said that two earlier cases, Roe versus Wade and Planned Parenthood versus Casey, were wrongly decided and must be overturned. The Court decided that the power to make abortion laws should be given back to the people and their elected representatives. Therefore, the Senate expresses deep gratitude to Mississippi State Representative Becky Currie for introducing the law that ultimately led to the historic victory of overturning Roe versus Wade and Planned Parenthood versus Casey. The Senate also honors states across the country that support life and have passed laws aimed at valuing and protecting the inherent dignity of every mother and unborn child.
